title: [Bug]: podman build --cache-from/to must contain registry and repository: invalid reference format
body:
### Issue Description

When running `podman build --layers --cache-from our.gitlab.registry.url/group/project/cache --cache-to our.gitlab.registry.url/group/project/cache ...` with podman 4.4.0 (homebrew) on a M1 Pro all I get is 

```
Error: failed to parse query parameter 'cacheFrom': "[\"our.gitlab.registry.url/group/project/cache\"]": invalid repo "[\"our.gitlab.registry.url/group/project/cache\"]": must contain registry and repository: invalid reference format
```

This happens with both flags, if I remove the one that is giving this error. 

I can pull and push from the registry using podman, which means the login and url is correct.

I also tried using eu.gcr.io/our-project/cache (also verifying it's credentials worked ok), but that gave the same error. 

Yesterday, before updating to 4.4.0, this worked ok. After upgrading to 4.4.0, I did podman machine stop+rm+init+start.

When I remove these flags alltogether, I get a successful build. 

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. I guess just any build with --cache-from/to on Mac with v4.4.0 podman via homebrew


### Describe the results you received

```
Error: failed to parse query parameter 'cacheFrom': "[\"our.gitlab.registry.url/group/project/cache\"]": invalid repo "[\"our.gitlab.registry.url/group/project/cache\"]": must contain registry and repository: invalid reference format
```

### Describe the results you expected

A container being built, with caches, without errors.

### Additional information

Additional information like issue happens only occasionally or issue happens with a particular architecture or on a particular setting

comments.body: A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
Looks like this no longer happens with Podman 4.4.2 on an M1.
